relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: The Eternal answered Job from the whirlwind.
The first question pierces human certainty: who darkens counsel without knowledge?
Job is called to stand and answer.
The Divine asks where Job was when earth was founded.
Measurement and design belong to a wisdom older than Job.
Earth’s foundations and cornerstone are beyond human memory.
Creation began in cosmic song and joy.
The sea burst forth like birth, yet was given doors.
Cloud and darkness clothed the newborn sea.
Limits, bars, and doors were set.
Even proud waves were given a boundary.
Morning and dawn answer a command Job never gave.
Dawn shakes the earth’s edges.
The world takes shape like clay under a seal.
Light is withheld from the violent arm.
The sea’s springs and deep recesses remain outside Job’s reach.
Death and deep shadow have gates Job has not mastered.
Earth’s breadth exceeds human grasp.
Light and darkness have dwellings beyond human command.
Their boundaries and pathways are not human possessions.
Divine irony exposes the smallness of human days.
Snow and hail have storehouses beyond sight.
They are kept for times of distress and conflict.
Light and east wind move by ways Job cannot assign.
Flood and thunderbolt have channels cut for them.
Rain falls even where no human lives.
Wilderness is satisfied for its own sake.
Rain and dew have origins beyond human parentage.
Ice and frost come from a hidden womb of creation.
Waters harden and the deep is held fast.
Pleiades and Orion are beyond human binding and loosening.
Constellations move in seasons not made by human hands.
Heavenly statutes rule beyond earthly control.
Clouds and floods do not answer human voice.
Lightning does not report to Job as servant.
Wisdom in the inward parts is a gift.
Clouds and heavenly jars cannot be counted or tipped by human wisdom.
Even dust and clods obey hidden processes.
The lion’s hunger is part of the wild order.
Young lions wait in dens beyond human management.
The raven’s young cry, and provision reaches even there.
